engineIntelligence.h2: Suzuki Kizashi Engines - Codes, Common Failures & Replacement Costs
engineIntelligence.description: The cards below cover the J24B engine fitted to the Suzuki Kizashi in the UK, including documented failure causes like cracked cylinder heads, variant production years, and typical rebuilt engine costs based on current UK market data.

cards.altText: Suzuki Kizashi J24B engine - petrol 2.4L replacement UK
productionHistory: Suzuki Kizashi S, SE, Sport, XLS - 2009-2016
Suzuki Kizashi CVT and manual variants - 2009-2016
knownFailures: Cracked cylinder head - early 2010-2012 units suffer casting defects causing coolant loss and overheating.
Porous engine block - manufacturing flaw on early J24B units leads to oil/coolant mixing.
Timing chain tensioner wear - rattling noise on startup, potential chain jump at high mileage.

cards.altText: Suzuki Kizashi J24B engine - petrol 2.4L replacement UK
productionHistory: Compatible with Grand Vitara J24B applications - cross-platform fitment verified
knownFailures: Coolant leak from rear of block - symptom of porous casting on 2010-2012 J24B units.
Oil consumption increase - worn piston rings on higher-mileage engines, upgraded rings available in rebuilt units.

cards.altText: Suzuki Kizashi J24B engine - petrol 2.4L replacement UK
productionHistory: Late-model J24B units (2013-2016) - improved casting quality, lower failure risk
Early J24B units (2010-2012) - higher risk of cylinder head/block defects
knownFailures: CVT transmission failure often mistaken for engine failure - gearbox replacement may be required alongside engine work.
VVT solenoid issues - variable valve timing faults cause rough idle and reduced power.
cards.replacementCost: Typical UK rebuilt engine: £1,850-£2,100 (supply only) Estimated from comparable J24B units - actual quotes may vary due to low-volume model scarcity.

commonProblems.h2: Suzuki Kizashi Engine Problems - What It Costs to Fix & When Replacement Makes Sense
commonProblems.h3: The Suzuki Kizashi's J24B 2.4L petrol engine is generally robust but early 2010-2012 units suffer documented casting defects affecting cylinder heads and blocks. Rebuilt J24B engines supplied through EngineMarket include upgraded piston rings and revised head gaskets designed to address original failure points - plus a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty.

problems.h4: Cracked Cylinder Head - Why Early J24B Engines Fail
problems.affectedModels: Suzuki Kizashi S, SE, Sport, XLS (2010-2012, J24B)
problems.rootCause: Casting defect in early J24B cylinder heads creates micro-fractures that expand under thermal stress, causing coolant leakage into combustion chambers.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace head gasket, machine head surface, pressure test
repairOptions.longevity: Temporary fix only if block is sound; not viable if head is cracked

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Fit reconditioned cylinder head, new gaskets, timing components
repairOptions.longevity: Viable if block is confirmed sound; requires specialist diagnosis

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt J24B with upgraded components, bench tested, 12-month warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Best value for Kizashi worth £6k-£7k; eliminates casting defect risk permanently

problems.recommendation: On a Kizashi valued at £6,000-£7,000, investing £1,850-£2,100 in a rebuilt J24B with upgraded internals makes far more sense than repeated head repairs. Early casting defects mean a cheap used replacement may fail the same way.
problems.cta: Compare rebuilt J24B engine prices for your Suzuki Kizashi

problems.h4: Porous Engine Block - Catastrophic J24B Failure
problems.rootCause: Porosity in early J24B block casting allows coolant and oil to mix, causing lubrication failure and potential engine seizure.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt J24B with revised block casting, upgraded components, 12-month warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Only viable solution; porous blocks cannot be reliably repaired

problems.recommendation: A porous block is a terminal failure. Do not attempt repair - source a rebuilt J24B with verified casting quality. Our network suppliers use late-production blocks or upgraded castings to prevent recurrence.
problems.cta: Get quotes for J24B Suzuki Kizashi replacement engine

problems.h4: Timing Chain Failure - J24B Rattle & Jump Risk
problems.rootCause: Timing chain tensioner wear allows chain slack, causing rattling on cold start; if ignored, chain can jump teeth causing valve/piston interference.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace timing chain, tensioner, guides, seals; reset timing
repairOptions.longevity: Viable if caught early before damage; recommended at 80k miles

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt J24B if chain jump caused valve damage
repairOptions.longevity: Required if interference damage occurred; rebuilt unit includes new chain kit

problems.recommendation: If you hear rattling on cold start but the engine still runs smoothly, a preventative chain kit replacement at £650-£950 is excellent value. If the chain has jumped, replacement is the only option.
problems.cta: Compare J24B timing chain replacement costs for your Kizashi

problems.h4: High Oil Consumption - J24B Piston Ring Wear
problems.affectedModels: Suzuki Kizashi high-mileage variants (80k+ miles, J24B)
problems.rootCause: Original J24B piston rings wear prematurely, allowing oil to enter combustion chambers; causes blue exhaust smoke and frequent top-ups.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Remove cylinder head, replace piston rings, hone bores, reassemble
repairOptions.longevity: Viable if block/head are sound; labour-intensive, specialist skill required

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt J24B with upgraded OEM piston rings (13150-87J00)
repairOptions.longevity: Often better value than ring replacement; includes warranty and all new components

problems.recommendation: For a Kizashi with 90k+ miles, the £1,850-£2,100 rebuilt engine with upgraded rings is frequently better value than a £1,200-£1,700 ring job on a worn engine. Our rebuilt units include the revised ring specification.
problems.cta: Find rebuilt J24B engines with upgraded piston rings for your Kizashi

problems.h4: CVT Transmission Failure - Often Mistaken for Engine Fault
problems.rootCause: CVT belt/variator wear causes slippage, shuddering, or loss of drive; symptoms often misdiagnosed as engine failure.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Disassemble gearbox, replace belt/variator, seals, fluid; reprogram TCU
repairOptions.longevity: Viable if engine is healthy; requires CVT specialist

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt J24B plus reconditioned CVT as bundled package
repairOptions.longevity: Best value if both components are high-mileage; single warranty, single installation

problems.recommendation: If your Kizashi CVT has 100k+ miles and the engine shows wear, our combined engine+CVT package at £2,800-£3,400 offers exceptional value versus separate repairs. Many owners opt for this preventative approach.
problems.cta: Enquire about Suzuki Kizashi engine + CVT replacement packages

finalCta.h4: Don't Let Engine Failure Write Off Your Suzuki Kizashi
finalCta.paragraph: The J24B's known casting defects and wear patterns are well-documented, but they don't have to mean scrapping your Kizashi. EngineMarket makes it simple to compare prices from trusted UK Suzuki Kizashi specialists who supply rebuilt engines with upgraded components addressing the original failure points. With nationwide delivery, supply & fit options, and a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty, you can get back on the road without the main dealer premium.
finalCta.buttonText: Compare Suzuki Kizashi Engine Replacement Prices Now →
finalCta.disclaimer: All prices are indicative UK market ranges based on historical quote data and repair requests from our network of engine specialists. Dealer pricing is estimated based on published main dealer labour rates and genuine OEM parts pricing. Specialist pricing reflects actual quote data from EngineMarket's UK network. Actual costs vary depending on damage severity, vehicle condition, variant, and workshop location within the UK. Use our free comparison tool to get tailored quotes for your specific Suzuki Kizashi and engine code. Every engine supplied through EngineMarket carries a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty - always confirm exact terms with your chosen supplier.

faq.h2: Suzuki Kizashi Engine Replacement - Frequently Asked Questions

items.question: How much does a Suzuki Kizashi engine replacement cost UK?
items.answer: A rebuilt Suzuki Kizashi J24B engine typically costs £1,850-£2,100 supply-only in the UK, with supply & fit adding £450-£750 depending on location and garage rates.
keyPoints: Rebuilt J24B: £1,850-£2,100 supply only
Used J24B: £950-£1,400 but higher risk on early units
Supply & fit adds £450-£750 to engine price
All rebuilt units include 12-month unlimited mileage warranty
items.cta: Compare rebuilt Suzuki Kizashi engine prices now

items.question: Will a Grand Vitara J24B engine fit my Suzuki Kizashi?
items.answer: Yes, the J24B engine code is identical across Suzuki Kizashi and Grand Vitara applications, making cross-platform compatibility verified for replacement purposes.
keyPoints: J24B engine code is identical on both models
Mounting points, ECU and wiring harness compatible
Verify donor vehicle year to avoid early casting defects
Cross-compatibility expands available donor pool significantly
items.cta: Get quotes for Grand Vitara J24B compatible with Kizashi

items.question: Why do Suzuki Kizashi engines fail?
items.answer: Early 2010-2012 J24B engines suffer documented casting defects affecting cylinder heads and blocks, causing coolant loss, overheating or oil/coolant mixing.
keyPoints: Casting defects on early J24B units cause cracked heads
Porous blocks allow oil/coolant mixing, terminal failure
Timing chain tensioner wear common at 90k+ miles
Late-production J24B (2013+) have improved casting quality
items.cta: Find rebuilt J24B engines with upgraded casting for your Kizashi

items.question: Is it worth replacing a Suzuki Kizashi engine?
items.answer: Yes, if your Kizashi is in good condition otherwise, a £1,850-£2,100 rebuilt J24B makes financial sense versus scrapping a £6,000-£7,000 vehicle.
keyPoints: Kizashi values £6k-£7k for good examples
Rebuilt J24B £1,850-£2,100 vs £5k+ main dealer
Upgraded components prevent repeat failure
12-month warranty protects your investment
items.cta: Calculate if engine replacement makes sense for your Kizashi

items.question: What is the most reliable Suzuki Kizashi engine?
items.answer: The J24B is the only engine fitted to the Kizashi; late-production units (2013+) have improved casting quality and lower failure risk than early 2010-2012 examples.
keyPoints: J24B is sole Kizashi engine across all variants
2013+ J24B units have revised casting specifications
Rebuilt J24B with upgraded components most reliable option
Regular servicing extends J24B lifespan significantly
items.cta: Compare late-model J24B availability for your Suzuki Kizashi

items.question: How long does a Suzuki Kizashi engine last?
items.answer: A well-maintained J24B can exceed 150,000 miles, but early 2010-2012 units with casting defects may fail between 60,000-90,000 miles without intervention.
keyPoints: Late-production J24B: 150k+ miles with proper care
Early units: casting defects cause premature failure 60k-90k miles
Regular oil changes critical for J24B longevity
Rebuilt units with upgraded components restore full lifespan
items.cta: Find long-life rebuilt J24B engines for your Kizashi

items.question: Can I upgrade my Suzuki Kizashi engine?
items.answer: The J24B is the only factory-fitted engine for the Kizashi; performance upgrades are limited, but rebuilt units with upgraded internals offer improved reliability over standard.
keyPoints: No factory alternative engine fits Kizashi chassis
Rebuilt J24B with upgraded rings/head gasket most practical upgrade
ECU remapping possible but limited gains on NA engine
Focus on reliability upgrades over power for daily use
items.cta: Enquire about upgraded-spec rebuilt J24B for your Kizashi

Single-point responsibility for supply and installation

items.question: What warranty comes with a Suzuki Kizashi replacement engine?
items.answer: Every rebuilt, reconditioned or remanufactured J24B engine supplied through EngineMarket includes a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty from UK specialists.
keyPoints: 12-month unlimited mileage warranty standard on rebuilt units
Warranty covers parts and labour for eligible failures
Terms vary by supplier; confirm before purchase
Warranty valid only with professional installation documentation
items.cta: Compare warranty terms on Suzuki Kizashi replacement engines

items.question: Are Suzuki Kizashi engines reliable?
items.answer: The J24B is generally robust when sourced from late-production or rebuilt with upgraded components, but early 2010-2012 units suffer documented casting defects requiring attention.
keyPoints: Late-production J24B (2013+) generally reliable with servicing
Early units have casting defects driving replacement demand
Rebuilt J24B with upgraded components most reliable option
Regular maintenance critical for J24B longevity
items.cta: Find reliable rebuilt J24B engines for your Suzuki Kizashi
